sourceMovie.title: Deliver Us from Evil
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Deliver Us from Evil (2020) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Death Wish
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Deliver Us from Evil clicked for you, Death Wish (1974) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Deliver Us from Evil clicked for you, The Last Days of American Crime (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Deliver Us from Evil?
faq.answer: Try Death Wish, The Enforcer, Harakiri—each shares DNA with Deliver Us from Evil while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Deliver Us from Evil?
faq.answer: Deliver Us from Evil (2020) is best described as Crime, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Deliver Us from Evil worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
